item.description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

media:content.media:title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’
media:content.media:description: President Trump sits down for an exclusive interview with Meet the Press as the U.S. looks to make a deal with Iran to end the war.

item.title: Exclusive: Trump says Iran hasn't made a deal because ‘they’re strong’ and ‘proud’

item.dc:creator: Freddie Clayton
item.description: Across the Middle East, three separate ceasefire deals are currently in effect.

media:content.media:text: Destruction after Israel's attack on a building in Gaza.
media:content.media:description: Palestinians retrieve some of their belongings that remained intact under the rubble after the Israeli army violated the ceasefire by targeting a building, which is owned by the Darwish family in the Nuseirat Camp, in Gaza, Palestine, on June 5, 2026.

item.title: Why ceasefires haven’t stopped deadly strikes in Gaza, Lebanon or the Gulf

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Israel faces mounting outrage over allegations of rape, beatings and humiliation of international activists detained on a flotilla attempting to break its naval blockade of Gaza.

media:content.media:text: A black eye can be seen on Suza's face
media:content.media:description: A man named Suza, one of the Americans who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla to Gaza, shows a bruise on his eye he said he received while in Israeli captivity, after returning to John F. Kennedy Airport, in New York City, on May 24, 2026.

media:content.media:text: People raise their fists as they walk past a crowd carrying Palestinian flags
media:content.media:description: Activist Saif Abukeshek, center, and fellow flotilla participants arrive at El Prat Airport, in Catalonia, Spain, on May 23.

media:content.media:text: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.
media:content.media:description: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.

media:content.media:text: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.

item.title: Israel faces growing pressure over alleged abuse of Gaza flotilla members

item.dc:creator: Gordon Lubold
Courtney Kube
Dan De Luce
item.description: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon is increasingly concerned about Israel ramping up its spying on the U.S., recently raising the counterintelligence threat level from America’s top ally in the Middle East to the highest level, according to two U.S. officials and one former U.S. official

media:content.media:text: Trump Mideast Wars Gaza
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu speak after Netanyahu addressed the Knesset, Israel's parliament, in October.

item.title: Pentagon raised threat of Israeli spying on U.S. to highest level, sources say

item.dc:creator: Jane Lytvynenko
item.description: Their appearance comes amid battlefield setbacks and dropping public sentiment in Russia.

media:content.media:text: Candace Owens and Andrew Tate have been posting from Russia as it hosts the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.
media:content.media:description: Candace Owens and Andrew Tate have been posting from Russia as it hosts the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.

item.title: Candace Owens and the Tate brothers turned up in Russia. The Kremlin spun it as a thaw.

item.dc:creator: Mirna Alsharif
item.description: The body of an Auburn University student who had been missing in Japan for a week has been found in a mountainous area outside of Kyoto, according to his family

media:content.media:text: Weston Higginbotham.
media:content.media:description: Weston Higginbotham.

item.title: Body of missing Auburn University student found in Japan

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Joe Murphy
item.description: The House issues a rare rebuke of the president, FIFA imposes a ban, and a flesh-eating parasite returns.
item.title: A ‘60 Minutes’ veteran gets fired, and a rookie hits an unusual home run: The news quiz

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Sebastian Berhalter’s path to the World Cup is not that of a young prodigy who had an easy way onto the U.S. men’s national team — despite what some might think of the son of storied former player and head coach Gregg Berhalter

media:content.media:text: Sebastian Berhalter dribbling a soccer ball during a game.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

media:content.media:text: Gregg Berhalter standing on the sidelines of a soccer field.
media:content.media:description: Sebastian Berhalter during a FIFA soccer match between Senegal and the USA in Charlotte, N.C., last month.

item.title: Sebastian Berhalter turned his father’s tough truth into World Cup breakthrough

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Claudio Lavanga
Mirna Alsharif
item.description: The United States has the papal blessing for the World Cup.

media:content.media:text: Pope Leo XIV.
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on June 3, 2026.

item.title: Pope Leo says he ‘certainly’ will support the U.S. in the World Cup

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: AI party

media:content.media:text: Second Annual AI Honors Hosted By The Washington AI Network
media:content.media:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, and Tammy Haddad speak onstage during the second-annual AI Honors hosted by the Washington AI Network on Wednesday.

item.title: AI’s elite celebrated in Washington as the public sours on data centers and chatbots

item.description: Across the Middle East, three separate ceasefire deals are currently in effect.

item.title: Body of missing Auburn University student found in Japan

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: The ad jingle you just can’t get out of your head — no matter how hard you may try — is staying on the air for now in California

media:content.media:text: Kars4Kids TV commercial.
media:content.media:description: Kars4Kids TV commercial.

item.title: Kars4Kids jingle can continue in California after appeals court ruling

item.dc:creator: Matt Lavietes
Maria Guerrero, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth
item.description: McKINNEY, Texas — A male student who witnessed the stabbing of 17-year-old Austin Metcalf testified at the Karmelo Anthony murder trial Saturday that wasn’t aware how badly Metcalf was injured until he lifted his shirt.“We only knew what happened when he lifted his shirt and saw a big hole,” the student testified

media:content.media:text: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments on June 4, 2026, in McKinney, Texas, during the trial of a teen accused of fatally stabbing another during a track meet in suburban Dallas last year.
media:content.media:description: A courtroom sketch shows the district attorney pointing at Karmelo Anthony, center, at the defense table in opening arguments in McKinney, Texas, on June 4 during the trial of a teen accused of fatally stabbing another during a track meet in suburban Dallas last year.

item.title: Witness testifies in Karmelo Anthony trial that victim lifted shirt to reveal ‘a big hole’

item.dc:creator: Keir Simmons
Natasha Lebedeva
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Russia — President Vladimir Putin looked out across a familiar scene on Friday — and a changing world

media:content.media:text: Russian President Vladimir Putin attends the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on June 5, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin attends the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Wide shot of the room as Russian President Vladimir Putin speaks at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Saint Petersburg on June 5, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin attends the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Russian forces launch drone strike on district in Zaporizhzhia
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin attends the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Russia's President Putin Attends The Eastern Economic Forum
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin attends the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Friday.

item.title: Russia opens door to ‘new relationship’ with U.S. as Putin wrestles with Ukraine deadlock

item.dc:creator: Claudio Lavanga
item.description: MADRID — Pope Leo may not find it hard to connect with his hosts as he kicks off a weeklong visit to Spain this weekend

media:content.media:text: Pope Leo.
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV waves upon his arrival at the Adolfo Suarez Madrid/Barajas International Airport on June 6.

item.title: Pope Leo praises Spain’s commitment to peace and respect for law after Trump rift

item.dc:creator: Jane Lytvynenko
item.description: Their appearance comes amid battlefield setbacks and dropping public sentiment in Russia.

media:content.media:text: Candace Owens and Andrew Tate have been posting from Russia as it hosts the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.
media:content.media:description: Candace Owens and Andrew Tate have been posting from Russia as it hosts the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.

item.title: Candace Owens and the Tate brothers turned up in Russia. The Kremlin spun it as a thaw.

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: For millions of people taking weight loss drugs, the next breakthrough may not be more weight loss.

media:content.media:text: Illustration of a calendar, with an image of an injectable GLP-1, with a red circle around it.
media:content.media:description: A monthly GLP-1 could cut the number of shots patients take each year from 52 to 12.

item.title: The next goal for weight loss drugs? Fewer injections

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: Israel faces mounting outrage over allegations of rape, beatings and humiliation of international activists detained on a flotilla attempting to break its naval blockade of Gaza.

media:content.media:text: A black eye can be seen on Suza's face
media:content.media:description: A man named Suza, one of the Americans who participated in the Global Sumud Flotilla to Gaza, shows a bruise on his eye he said he received while in Israeli captivity, after returning to John F. Kennedy Airport, in New York City, on May 24, 2026.

media:content.media:text: People raise their fists as they walk past a crowd carrying Palestinian flags
media:content.media:description: Activist Saif Abukeshek, center, and fellow flotilla participants arrive at El Prat Airport, in Catalonia, Spain, on May 23.

media:content.media:text: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.
media:content.media:description: A video posted by Israeli Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir shows flotilla members in what appears to be a makeshift detention area at Ashdod port.

media:content.media:text: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.
media:content.media:description: Ben-Gvir, left, addresses a Gaza activist who has his hands tied and is kneeling on the floor.

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Joe Murphy
item.description: The House issues a rare rebuke of the president, FIFA imposes a ban, and a flesh-eating parasite returns.
item.title: A ‘60 Minutes’ veteran gets fired, and a rookie hits an unusual home run: The news quiz

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
Scott Wong
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is seeking to put his stamp on the city’s congressional delegation with a trio of endorsements against incumbent lawmakers and their picks, a move that could further expand the power of his democratic socialist movement or thwart its progress — and dent his own image in the process.“This is high-risk, high reward,” said Rebecca Katz, a prominent Democratic strategist and ally whose firm worked with Mamdani during his 2025 run.

media:content.media:text: Zohran Mamdani, left, and Brad Lander speak into press microphones outside
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

media:content.media:text: Darializa Avila Chevalier smiles for a portrait
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

media:content.media:text: Claire Valdez speaks outside in front of people holding signs
media:content.media:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former New York City Comptroller Brad Lander.

item.title: Mamdani makes ‘high-risk, high-reward’ move to reshape New York’s voice in Washington

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
item.description: Hundreds of supporters signaled they weren’t phased by the new allegations about Graham Platner’s past relationships, giving the Democratic Senate candidate a warm welcome on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Graham Platner
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate, speaks at a campaign event Friday, June 5, 2026, in Bar Harbor, Maine.

item.title: ‘Maine had my back’: Graham Platner hits the campaign trail amid latest turmoil

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: The zoo this week launched an online petition against the data center that, as of Friday, had more than 180,000 signatures and 25,000 shares on Facebook.

media:content.media:text: Sumatran tiger cubs
media:content.media:description: Sumatran tiger cubs explore the tiger exhibit at the Nashville Zoo in Nashville, Tenn.

item.title: Nashville Zoo tries to halt data center project next door over animal safety concerns
